content: Hey, I'm thinking of hosting a 3D printing workshop at my house in Chicago. Do you think that's a good idea?
That sounds like a great idea! What kind of projects were you thinking of having people work on?
I was thinking of having them print out some custom phone cases and maybe some small decorative items. I've been getting into 3D printing lately and I think it's really cool.
That sounds like a lot of fun. Have you thought about how you're going to promote the workshop and get people to attend?
Yeah, I was thinking of posting about it on social media and maybe reaching out to some local meetup groups. I've also been talking to my friend, Alex, about helping me promote it.
That's a good plan. What kind of experience do you have with 3D printing? Are you going to be teaching people how to use the printers or just providing the materials?
I've been doing it for about a year now, so I feel pretty comfortable with it. I'll be teaching people how to use the printers and providing the materials.
That's great. I've heard that 3D printing can be really challenging, so it's good that you have some experience. Do you have any other hobbies or interests aside from 3D printing?
Yeah, I'm really into aquariums and I've been keeping fish for about 5 years now. I also love playing chess and hiking.
That's cool. I've heard that aquariums can be really relaxing. Do you have a favorite type of fish to keep?
Yeah, I love keeping cichlids. They're so interesting to watch and they're really intelligent.
That's great. I'll have to look into that. So, what made you decide to move to Chicago from the Bay Area?
I just needed a change of pace and I found a great opportunity to start my own electrician business here. Plus, the cost of living is much lower, so it's been a great decision for me.
That makes sense. It can be tough to adjust to a new city, but it sounds like you're really enjoying Chicago. Do you have any fun plans for the summer?
I hope you have a great time at the workshop and with your other plans
